originalAlarm.description: Value stored to '__info_1612' is never read

sanitizedDescription: Value stored to 'info' is never read
presenceCondition:
(let ((a!1 (or (and (and (not _STDLIB_H)
                         __need_malloc_and_calloc
                         BB_FEATURE_LS_FILETYPES)
                    BB_FEATURE_LS_USERNAME)
               (and (and _STDLIB_H BB_FEATURE_LS_FILETYPES)
                    BB_FEATURE_LS_USERNAME))))
  (and (or (and (not _STDLIB_H)
                __need_malloc_and_calloc
                BB_FEATURE_LS_FILETYPES)
           (and _STDLIB_H BB_FEATURE_LS_FILETYPES))
       a!1))
model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  true)
(define-fun BB_FEATURE_LS_USERNAME () Bool
  true)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun BB_FEATURE_LS_FILETYPES () Bool
  true)
reasoning: info is assigned at L11 when BB_FEATURE_LS_USERNAME=T, but it IS conditionally read at L16 (printf("%d", info)) inside the BB_FEATURE_LS_FILETYPES block when rand()%2 is true. The dead store alarm is a false positive — info is read on at least one execution path. The known VBDB bug (// ERROR at L16) is an uninitialized read when FILETYPES=T AND !USERNAME, which is a distinct alarm type.
